human:
POINTS:
- [flink-table/flink-table-api-java/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/table/api/internal/ShowCreateUtil.java:180] would be great to a have a test verifying it works
probably `ShowCreateUtilTest`
v2:
SUMMARY:
This PR adds an 8-arg overload of `buildShowCreateMaterializedTableRow` with `includeFreshness` and `includeRefreshMode` flags, allowing callers to suppress `FRESHNESS` and `REFRESH_MODE` clauses from `SHOW CREATE MATERIALIZED TABLE` output. The 6-arg overload delegates with `true, true`, preserving backward compatibility.

POINTS:
- [flink-table/flink-table-api-java/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/table/api/internal/ShowCreateUtil.java:147-155] The new 8-arg overload has no Javadoc. The 6-arg overload has `/** Show create materialized table statement only for materialized tables. */`; consider adding similar (or a delegating) javadoc to the 8-arg method for discoverability.

- [flink-table/flink-table-api-java/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/table/api/internal/ShowCreateUtil.java:180-186] The conditional `if` blocks for `includeFreshness` and `includeRefreshMode` correctly avoid emitting trailing blank lines when both flags are `false` — the newline after `START_MODE` is unconditional, and each clause appends its own trailing `\n` inside the guard. Good.

- [flink-table/flink-table-api-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/table/api/internal/ShowCreateUtilTest.java:150-193] The new `@ParameterizedTest` with `@CsvSource` covers all 4 boolean combinations (true/true, true/false, false/true, false/false). Clean incremental expected-string construction matching the conditional emission logic. Good test design.

- [flink-table/flink-table-api-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/table/api/internal/ShowCreateUtilTest.java:130-147] The existing `showCreateMaterializedTable` test calls the 6-arg overload, which now delegates to the 8-arg with `true, true`. All 5 parameterized cases in `argsForShowCreateMaterializedTable()` expect both `FRESHNESS =` and `REFRESH_MODE =` lines, so backward compatibility is fully exercised. Good.

- [flink-table/flink-table-api-java-uber/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/table/api/internal/ShowCreateMaterializedTableOperation.java:62] The sole production caller uses the 6-arg overload and has no configuration knob to wire `includeFreshness`/`includeRefreshMode`. The PR description targets "deployments that fix freshness or refresh mode at the platform level" — the API is ready, but the integration point in `ShowCreateMaterializedTableOperation` is not wired yet. This is acceptable for an `@Internal` utility method (preparatory work), but be aware that the feature is dormant until a caller adopts the 8-arg overload.

- [flink-table/flink-table-api-java/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/table/api/internal/ShowCreateUtil.java] This flag-based conditional-override pattern has no precedent among the other `buildShowCreate*Row` methods (`buildShowCreateTableRow`, `buildShowCreateViewRow`, `buildShowCreateModelRow`, `buildShowCreateCatalogRow` all have single signatures). This is fine, but the pattern is novel within this class.
v3:
SUMMARY:
This PR adds an 8-arg overload of `buildShowCreateMaterializedTableRow` with `includeFreshness` and `includeRefreshMode` flags to conditionally suppress FRESHNESS/REFRESH_MODE clauses from SHOW CREATE output. The existing 6-arg overload delegates with `true, true`, preserving backward compatibility. Implementation is clean and test coverage is complete.

POINTS:
- [flink-table/flink-table-api-java/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/table/api/internal/ShowCreateUtil.java:148] The new 8-arg public overload is missing Javadoc. The original 6-arg method has only a brief `/** Show create materialized table statement only for materialized tables. */` comment. Since this new overload is `public` (even though the class is `@Internal`), add Javadoc documenting what `includeFreshness` and `includeRefreshMode` control and the intended usage scenario (platform-level deployments that fix these values externally).
- [flink-table/flink-table-api-java/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/table/api/internal/ShowCreateUtil.java:181-185] Note (non-blocking): This PR allows suppressing FRESHNESS and REFRESH_MODE but there is no corresponding flag for START_MODE, which remains always-emitted (line 177). If a platform-level caller wants to suppress all three clauses, they currently cannot. Consider whether START_MODE should also receive a flag for symmetry, or document this asymmetry explicitly.
- [flink-table/flink-table-api-java/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/table/api/internal/ShowCreateUtil.java:177-185] Minor observation: When both `includeFreshness` and `includeRefreshMode` are `false`, the output transitions directly from `START_MODE = ...\n` to `AS <query>\n` with no blank line — this is clean and produces valid SQL. The conditional logic correctly avoids emitting empty lines.
- [flink-table/flink-table-api-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/table/api/internal/ShowCreateUtilTest.java:151-192] Good: The `@CsvSource({"true, true", "true, false", "false, true", "false, false"})` parameterized test comprehensively covers all flag combinations. Existing tests that exercise the 6-arg overload are unaffected by the delegation.
v4:
SUMMARY:
This PR adds an 8-arg overload of `buildShowCreateMaterializedTableRow` with `includeFreshness` and `includeRefreshMode` flags, allowing callers to suppress those clauses from SHOW CREATE output. The existing 6-arg overload delegates to the new one with `true, true`, preserving backward compatibility. One production caller (`ShowCreateMaterializedTableOperation`) uses the 6-arg version and is unaffected.

POINTS:
- [flink-table/flink-table-api-java/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/table/api/internal/ShowCreateUtil.java:146] The new 8-arg overload lacks Javadoc for the two new boolean parameters. Add `@param` tags explaining `includeFreshness` and `includeRefreshMode` and document the intended use case (platform-level deployments that fix these values at the platform level).
- [flink-table/flink-table-api-java/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/table/api/internal/ShowCreateUtil.java:146] The 6-arg overload is `public static` but the new 8-arg overload is package-private. Consider making the 8-arg overload `public` as well so that external platform integrations (the stated use case) can invoke it directly without reflection.
- [flink-table/flink-table-api-java/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/table/api/internal/ShowCreateUtil.java:180-185] When both flags are `false`, the output will contain an extra blank line between `START_MODE` and `AS` (since `extractStartMode` always appends `"\n"` and the conditionals contribute nothing). Consider whether a trailing blank line in the rendered DDL is desirable, or whether the newline after START_MODE should also be conditional on at least one of the flags being true.
- [flink-table/flink-table-api-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/table/api/internal/ShowCreateUtilTest.java:150] Good use of `@CsvSource` to cover all four combinations of the two boolean flags — clean and concise parameterization.
- [flink-table/flink-table-api-java/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/table/api/internal/ShowCreateUtil.java:180] The conditional emission pattern is clean and straightforward. No issues with the logic itself.
